excerpt.variant_perception:
**Market believes:** Tencent is a recovering China internet conglomerate — gaming approvals have normalised, ad revenue is rebounding on AI-driven targeting, and the buyback programme provides a valuation floor. The investment portfolio is a mixed bag of stakes that may or may not unlock value. The stock is fairly valued, pricing in a mature, regulated platform with mid-single-digit growth.

**Why wrong:** The market conflates regulatory-cycle normalisation with secular platform deepening. Weixin's ad load remains "much lower than peers'" (management's own words) — this is not a recovery story, it is structural under-monetisation of a 1.4bn-MAU distribution platform. As Video Accounts, Mini Programs, and AI-targeted ads layer onto this base, revenue per user can compound at low-double-digits for years without aggressive ad-load increases. The market prices 8-10% growth; the platform can deliver 12-15% owner-earnings growth on a normalised basis once SBC moderates.

**What proves us wrong:** (a) SBC growth does not moderate — if SBC/revenue crosses 6%+ by FY27, the compounder is structurally broken regardless of top-line performance. (b) Ad-load expansion fails to materialise — if Marketing Services growth reverts to single digits for two consecutive quarters despite the AI targeting rollout, the monetisation headroom thesis is falsified. (c) Regulatory re-freeze — a new gaming license suspension materially impairs Domestic Games, the largest VAS profit driver.

**Market believes:** the -60%+ round-trip from the 2024 high reflects a structural break — Novo's obesity/diabetes near-duopoly is being dismantled by Lilly's oral and injectable competition, CagriSema's REDEFINE 1 data (22.7% weight loss) was a disappointment, and US pricing/political pressure (MFN, 340B, compounding) will impair growth and margins indefinitely, justifying a re-rating from a >30x growth multiple to a mid-teens "ex-growth pharma" multiple.

**Why wrong (partially):** the market is conflating a genuine, GUIDED, one-to-two-year PRICE/rebate-mechanics reset with a volume/demand collapse. Adjusted Diabetes care sales fell -12% CER in Q1 2026, but that is driven "mainly by lower realised prices" (MFN, gross-to-net normalization), not primarily by volume loss — while adjusted Obesity care sales grew +22% CER in the SAME quarter, with the newly-launched Wegovy oral pill already contributing DKK 2,256m in its first three months on the market [Q1 2026 announcement, "Adjusted sales" table]. CagriSema's 22.7% weight loss at 68 weeks beat semaglutide alone (16.1%) convincingly and was submitted to the FDA in Dec 2025 — a miss against an informally inflated ~25% bar, not a failed asset. Amycretin (Phase 3 obesity program starting Q1 2026, ~22% weight loss at 36wk in Phase 1b/2a) offers a second, underappreciated re-acceleration catalyst. Global GLP-1/obesity market penetration remains low relative to the eligible population, so the category is not shrinking — Novo's…

**Market believes:** Sony has completed its transformation into a content-and-recurring-revenue compounder, with PlayStation platform economics, music catalog scarcity, and a leading image-sensor position justifying a premium multiple. The SFGI spin-off simplified the structure, and the ongoing buyback signals management confidence in undervaluation.
**Why wrong:** The spin-off extracted the easy discount. What remains is governance-structural — cross-subsidization across segments, conglomerate overhead, and no pressure to break up further. Markets price buybacks at announcement, not at completion — the ¥500bn authorization is already in the price. Without a forward catalyst to unlock residual value, the discount can persist indefinitely. G&NS underlying OI is flat (the ¥57.2bn FX/impairment comparison padding masks this). I&SS faces discrete socket-loss risk, not a smooth fade.
**What proves us wrong:** (1) No further restructuring, spin-off, or activist pressure announced within 12 months — discount persists or widens. (2) Apple formally dual-sources image sensors, hitting I&SS revenue >15%. (3) G&NS OI declines as the FX tailwind and Bungie-impairment comparison benefits reverse in FYE-Mar-2027.

**Market believes:** The multi-year capex-driven margin/FCF trough (2023-2025) is over; the Feb-2026 Capital Management Day guidance (capex cut to $2-3bn, FCF/share target >$8) confirms an inflection, and TI deserves a re-rated multiple as a widening-moat 300mm-cost-position compounder — a view the market has already substantially acted on (+32%/12m, +67%/6m, screen row market-data snapshot).

**Why wrong:** We do not dispute the mechanism — it is real, filing-confirmed (Embedded op margin 6.2%→16.9% YoY in Q1'26 alone), and our own bottom-up build (constructed before viewing price) supports the DIRECTION of the consensus story. Where we differ is magnitude/price, not direction — that is a thin edge and we say so plainly. Running our own reinvestment-rate × ROIIC framework at a disciplined 9% COE and 2.5% terminal g (moat-adjusted slow fade to year ~15), fair EV/EBIT works out to **~12-14x**. At last price $311.46, EV (mkt cap $284.7bn + net debt $9.2bn = $293.9bn) is **~40x** our FY26E operating profit ($7,279m) and still **~29x** our FY29E operating profit ($10,187m) — a genuine, filings-derived gap, not a "feels expensive" comment, and one that persists even under our reasonably bullish forward margin path. The market may be paying for a longer/stronger fade than we think prudent, and appears to be underweighting that ~50% of TXN revenue ships into China (10-K geographic note) against the current "China weak/un-reflating" macro read…

**Market believes:** Astra International is a permanent value trap — a slow-growing conglomerate proxy where the 30%+ holdco discount is structural and irreversible because Jardine's 50.1% control blocks any value-unlocking catalyst, and BEV disruption threatens the Toyota/Daihatsu-anchored auto distribution earnings base.
**Why wrong:** I cannot defend this line. The Bear is correct: the discount IS the equilibrium. Without a governance catalyst that Jardine itself would initiate, there is no mechanism for the discount to narrow. A 4% buyback is immaterial against a 20–40pp structural gap. The consensus is not wrong — it is the base-rate reality.
**What proves us wrong:** The holdco discount would need to narrow meaningfully without a governance catalyst — which means the thesis was wrong from inception. The price action that would "prove us wrong" is exactly the base-case path: discount persists or widens, UNTR/AALI commodity earnings erode SOTP further, and Jardine maintains the status quo.

**Market believes:** The ~30% Apr->Jul 2026 slide (Rp1,665-1,670 in April to Rp1,170 now) is simply beta to the IDX small-cap/foreign-outflow washout (CNBC Indonesia: Rp22tn foreign net sell in March 2026; weak rupiah; the macro brief's BoP-pressure regime) — nothing company-specific to re-price.

**Why wrong:** FY2025 audited results and the 9-Jun-2026 RUPST — filed in a 370-page report and Indonesian-language trade press a thin analyst base is unlikely to have fully digested — show a materially improved company: EBITDA +31.7% to a record, NI +60.2%, net-debt/equity nearly halved, dividend nearly doubled (Rp24->Rp47). The stock kept falling *through* the RUPST/dividend window into July — the print didn't stop the slide, consistent with slow LK-detail incorporation in a ~Rp8.6bn/day name, not with the market having processed and rejected the improved fundamentals.

**What proves us wrong:** If H1/Q2 2026 results (due ~30-Aug-2026) show FFB/CPO deceleration, OER's slide becoming a genuine cost/quality problem, or the stock fails to stabilize even after that print, that confirms the market's read (deserved de-rating, no discovery catalyst) over ours.
excerpt.kill_criteria:
I independently verified the primary filings (indopremier FY2025 statements, haisawit/bisnis/palmoilmagazine/idnfinancials trade press, RUPST releases, stockanalysis/investing.com balance-sheet data, TradingView/Kontan price data). Three of the trade's load-bearing claims survive; two do not, the catalyst logic is factually inverted, and the sizing math fails its own EV test.

**0. Fact-check of the three load-bearing numbers.**
- *Net income +60.2% (Rp1.84tn attributable, +61.1%): CONFIRMED.* Rp1,840.5bn vs Rp1,142.5bn FY24, EPS Rp173.63 → trailing P/E 6.74x at Rp1,170. Solid. (indopremier FY2025; haisawit; palmoilmagazine.)
- *Dividend Rp47/sh, total Rp498.2bn, RUPST 9-Jun-2026: CONFIRMED*, "highest in 5 years" (idnfinancials), prior FY24 Rp24 — near-doubling holds. But this is now a SPENT catalyst: cum 18-Jun, paid 8-Jul — as of today (11-Jul) the stock is ex-dividend and the yield-buyers have their cash.
- *EBITDA +31.7% to Rp4,077,998mn / 33.1% margin: NOT CONFIRMED — margin materially overstated.* Two independent third-party computations put FY25 EBITDA at ~Rp2,895bn / **23.5% margin** (indopremier line-item; haisawit "EBITDA margin 23,5%"). Your headline Rp4,078bn (33.1%) EXCEEDS reported gross profit (Rp3,850bn, 31.3%) and implies D&A of ~Rp1,249bn — yet your OWN appendix NAV build uses D&A ≈Rp724bn and a through-cycle EBITDA basis of ~Rp2,663bn (≈23%). You cannot reconcile your headline with your own valuation inputs. The +~32% GROWTH direction is real; the…

excerpt.variant_perception:
**Market believes:** ELSA is a generic small-cap Indonesian O&G price-beta name riding a momentum pop (it screens WASHOUT/NEGLECTED/MOMENTUM simultaneously) — that the +43.5% 6m / +17.6% 1m re-rating is speculative, flow- or PDSI-merger-rumor-driven, into a softening-Brent backdrop (Brent down from ~$117 April-2026 peak to ~$85 June-2026, EIA forecasting further easing toward ~$74 in Q3), and will fade once oil sentiment cools.

**Why wrong:** Elnusa's addressable market is not primarily a spot-Brent bet. It runs off SKK Migas's multi-year, government-mandated upstream *activity* program (the "Triple 100" well-count target and the 1-million-BOPD production goal), budgeted on a multi-year PSC/cost-recovery cycle that is lagged relative to, not synchronous with, month-to-month Brent moves. Elnusa's own disclosed upstream contract backlog has more than **doubled year-on-year** (~Rp4.4tn → ~Rp9.9tn, 9M2024 vs 9M2025) — a real, filing-adjacent operational data point that predates and is largely independent of the 1-month price pop, and that thin sell-side coverage (screen tags this NEGLECTED rank-1) has plausibly not fully modeled. The dividend (45% payout, well-covered >4x by through-cycle FCF) provides a valuation floor a pure price-beta framing ignores.

**What proves us wrong:** A sustained decline in disclosed upstream contract backlog (kill kpi below); Brent staying under ~$70 for several quarters actually stalling the 2026 well-count program (evidence: KKKS deferring…

**Market believes:** JSMR is a stable, state-backed toll-road compounder with an expanding 1,736km network, mechanical inflation-linked biennial tariff resets, and improving leverage post-JTT stake sale — a bond-proxy with growth optionality in Indonesia's infrastructure buildout.

**Why wrong:** The funding-cost/escalator mismatch is acute and active. JSMR's Rp10.6tn floating-rate bank debt reprices quarterly while biennial tariffs reset on 2-year lagged CPI — BI's defensive hiking cycle (5.75%, chatter to 6%) compresses real AFFO faster than tariff resets can recover it. This is exactly the archetype's dominant failure mode, not a future tail risk. The JTT stake sale ceded 35% of permanent toll cash flows without fixing the structural mismatch — deleveraging was cosmetic, not organic. Best-effort AFFO cannot clear the 10y FR 7.26% hurdle.

**What proves us wrong:** BI reverts to neutral/accommodative by H2 2027; tariff reform accelerates reset cadence; asset recycling at premium valuations eliminates floating-rate exposure; or traffic growth surprises materially above GDP.

**Market believes:** PNBN is a structurally low-ROE, thinly-traded, family-controlled bank in a multi-year downtrend (mom12 -17.07%, cagr -14.35%/yr) that deserves its sub-0.4x P/BV, and that the unresolved ANZ/Gunawan ownership overhang plus a rising 9M2025 provisioning line (+35.1% yoy) are reasons to avoid it until the picture clears — exactly the kind of name thin sell-side coverage lets sit unrepriced in either direction.
**Why wrong:** The magnitude of the price move (-25.96% off the 52w high) is running well ahead of the magnitude of the fundamental move over the comparable window — NIM fell 61bp (4.93%→4.32%, FY2023→FY2025) and ROE fell 54bp (5.42%→4.88%), while the bank stayed solidly profitable, kept a ~35-36% dividend payout intact, improved NPL gross every year (3.53%→2.82%), improved BOPO back to 70.22% after a one-off H1'24 opex spike, and sits on a 37.07% CAR with no notation/manipulation-class governance flag found. Thin coverage means no one is marking this to a normalized-cycle model — the tape is extrapolating the trailing quarter's rising provisioning line without weighing it against the (also real) capital fortress and maintained payout. **Important honesty check:** my own VAL_BANKS math does NOT call PNBN cheap vs COE (warranted P/BV 0.20-0.27x =3, observable, current, each citing a dossier/filing line)
1. NPL net rose from 0.90% (FY2024) to 1.04% (FY2025) even as NPL gross "improved" from 3.05% to 2.82% over the same year — coverage is thinning, not…
